channel.title: Comments for page "Your Call Is Important To Us"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Your Call Is Important To Us"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
“MY GOD THERE’S BLOOD EVERYWHERE! Mostly inside us, but still: THE HORROR!”

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now this is clearly the work of someone who has spent waaaaaaaaaay too long in the service industry. Get out while you still can!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bailey told me that Canada has glow-in-the-dark toonies and I’ll be damned if a semi-real country has more whimsical currency than we do.

As a Canadian who has a thing for novelty coins (don't ask), I was laughing so damn hard.
Those circus tokens are probably worth way more than our stupid metal ducks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’m reading Dread & Circuses in sequence from the beginning and until this piece it seemed like it was trying too hard, overblowing with the gore and unlikability of the characters.
But this was amazing. It was nice to see a refreshing look at a GoI coming from the telephone guy. The interplay with other SCPs was very well written too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has to be one of my favorite romps through this universe, and especially in your Dread & Circuses tale series. Not many things on this site (well, this can be expanded to all written work) make me laugh out loud just reading them. This did. "This is a drill! Do not panic, this is a drill!" … "Oh god it's not a drill! I repeat, this is not a drill! Everybody panic!"

That and everything else just really got me. Very nice. Very, very nice.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
This was requested on my channel and it's one of the most fun recordings I've ever put together! Thank you so much for this awesome tale!

https://youtu.be/g9dp0V2mkLs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Balloon guy, balloon guy
He's that tiny speck in the sky
Can he bite through a thread
If he can't, he'll be dead
Look out!
Please look out for balloon guy and let him know we're thinking of him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
♫ Balloon guy, balloon guy, trying very hard not to die.
Getting caught, in spider webs
Who can tell, what happens next?
Look ouuuuut
Here comes the balloon guy. ♫

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Telephonesis.
The superpower you never knew you wanted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was irresistibly reminded of my years in the civil service. Excellent customer service, Gary!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Balloon guy is the hero we need.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always love a good madcap romp through the SCP-verse, and this one is extremely well done- great balance of serious and silly that fits the Fuller vibe perfectly.

The inclusion of SCP-1506 was brilliant, btw. I actually hope balloon guy did just drop his phone because I kind of want to see more of his hapless misadventures in scipland.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As mentioned, it can be quite entertaining. Some of it can get a bit annoying, but I didn't let that stop my enjoyment of the overall.

item.title: Oh may this tale get over 9000 likes
item.content:encoded: I always love the disquieting circus tales, and this one was so well rounded, incorporating so much of the Fuller universe. I can only hope to read much more of your work very soon. You nailed it.
